description: Enforceability-dimension lexicons for the folklore check (epistemics feature #1). Classifies each rule by HOW a violation could be detected: enforceable (a runnable check), observable (a self-checkable structural directive), or folklore (an unverifiable quality word with no checkable referent). REUSES verbs.json (directive verbs), markers.json (abstract_markers = quality words, concrete markers/terms = checkable referents) and framing.json elsewhere in the pipeline; this file adds ONLY the tokens those lexicons do not already carry: enforcement-command cues and gate keywords. Conservative by design — folklore requires BOTH an unverifiable quality word AND the absence of any checkable token.

enforcement_command_markers.description: Phrases that name a runnable check / external gate. Presence of any of these (or a backtick command, a numeric threshold, or a concrete tool/path from markers.json) makes a rule ENFORCEABLE — a hook, linter, test, build gate, or Hestia probe could mechanically detect a violation.
enforcement_command_markers.note: Lowercased substring match against the rule text. Backtick-wrapped tokens, numeric thresholds (markers.json::numeric_threshold_regex), and concrete tool names (markers.json::concrete_regex framework list) are ALSO enforceable signals but are detected via the existing markers.json lexicon rather than duplicated here.

quality_words.description: Unverifiable quality words with no checkable referent. A rule that hinges on one of these AND names no concrete construct is FOLKLORE. The primary source is markers.json::abstract_markers (reused at runtime, not duplicated). This list adds only quality words / hedges the spec names that abstract_markers does not already carry.
quality_words.note: markers.json::abstract_markers already covers clean, maintainable, readable, proper(ly), correct(ly), good, appropriate, reasonable, careful(ly), as needed, when possible, where practical, simple, clear, etc. Loaded together at runtime so both lexicons are versioned data, not hardcoded .py lists.
